channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Protests against Trump and Project 2025 are planned in cities across the US
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge to hear arguments in suit aiming to stop Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday is set to hear arguments over temporarily pausing President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas man set to be executed for killing a pastor in his own church
item.description: A Texas man convicted of beating and suffocating a Dallas area pastor in his own church during a robbery was facing execution on Wednesday, the second scheduled execution in the U.S. so far this year. Steven Lawayne Nelson was condemned for the 2011 killing of Rev.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DeSantis goes to battle with Florida Republicans in trying to get closer to Trump
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — With an open presidential primary coming in 2028, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is launching an offensive to win back supporters of the Republican leader whom he dared to challenge in the last election.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order barring transgender female athletes from competing
item.description: President Donald Trump will sign an executive order on Wednesday designed to prevent people who were biologically assigned male at birth from participating in women’s or girls' sporting events.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: Almost a week after a helicopter and airplane collision in Washington killed 67 people, investigators are still at work as salvage crews pull out chunks of aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration will consider redrawing boundaries of national monuments as part of energy push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As part of the Trump administration’s push to expand U.S. energy production, federal officials will review and consider redrawing the boundaries of national monuments created under previous presidents to protect unique landscapes and cultural resources.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Still locked out of federal funding, several Head Start preschools may need to close temporarily
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start preschools serving thousands of children around the country could be at risk of closing because they remain locked out of their federal funding, a problem that first surfaced last week during President Donald Trump's aborted effort to freeze federal grants.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks former chief entangled in 'Sharpiegate' to lead NOAA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has nominated Neil Jacobs to lead the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, turning to the man who was his acting NOAA chief in 2019 when Trump altered an expected hurricane impact map in what became known as the “Sharpiegate” scandal.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egg heist: Pennsylvania police investigating after 100,000 eggs stolen
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police investigating after 100,000 eggs were allegedly stolen from an organic farm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former paraprofessional sentenced to 4 years for sexual assault of student
item.description: Imagine Ewer, 28, pleaded guilty to one charge of first-degree contributing to the delinquency of a minor and one charge of sexual assault on a child by one in a position of trust.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Cartel war escalates, creates new threat at southern border
item.description: Border agents are also under threat, with many at the border feeling unsettled by current events as cartels become more desperate.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests displaced Palestinians in Gaza be 'permanently' resettled outside war-torn territory
item.description: The renewed call by the Trump administration on Arab nations to relocate displaced Palestinians comes as Trump is set to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House later Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: District judge rules New Mexico schools are not required to follow 180 day rule
item.description: Under the state's public school code, the school year is supposed to contain at least 1,140 hours but there's no requirement for the number of days.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Hoops, NewsNation

item.title: Waffle House adding surcharge for every egg ordered
item.description: Waffle House restaurants across the nation added a 50-cent surcharge to every egg ordered amid soaring prices due to inflation and the bird flu epidemic.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: DC plane crash: Airport employees arrested over leaked video of midair collision
item.description: Two Washington, D.C., airport employees have been arrested in connection with the leak of a video showing the moment Flight 5342 collided with a Black Hawk helicopter on Jan. 29.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Philadelphia boy praised as hero for shielding sister during plane crash
item.description: A grade schooler is being credited for saving his sister from falling debris during the northeast Philadelphia plane crash on Jan. 31, 2025 that left seven people dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street falls following Trump's tariffs, but not as badly as feared in the morning
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The threat of a punishing trade war sent Wall Street on a roller coaster Monday. After initially falling sharply on worries about President Donald Trump’s tariffs, U.S. stocks pared their losses after Mexico said it had negotiated a one-month reprieve.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Marco Rubio taking over as acting head of USAID amid agency turmoil
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is being put in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) as the Trump administration mulls the future of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fans at Raptors game continue trend of booing US national anthem at pro sporting events in Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Fans at a Toronto Raptors game continued an emerging trend Sunday of booing the American national anthem at pro sporting events in Canada.
item.dc:creator: IAN HARRISON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Journaling is hard, but this smart new app makes it easier
item.description: Keeping a journal isn't easy, but the benefits are clear. "The need we have to try to make sense or our experience is deep," said David Coates, a psychotherapist and coach.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chappell Roan wins best new artist Grammy Award
item.description: Chappell Roan nudged aside Sabrina Carpenter to win the Grammy for best new artist Sunday, capping a remarkably explosive rise to fame for an artist dropped by her last record label.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Airport employee injured in collision with aircraft wing at Chicago's O'Hare
item.description: The driver of a tug vehicle at Chicago O'Hare International Airport was injured Saturday evening after colliding with the wing of an airplane.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5
